Resolve the attack by rolling number of damage dice equal t0 the meters fallen with an AP of 0 and melee dam age type (M} If the falling character has Acrobatics or Free Fall Skill; successful Skill Check lowers the effect of the fall by one wound level (from Minor to Grazing; for example).
BURPRISE Circumstances may arise which the target completely unaware 0f the attack coming at him; The gamemaster must determine surprise conditions exist; as general rule, they require the right kind of battlefield (cover and/or low light to hide in) and successful Opposed Action Check, using the attacking character" Stealth Skill against tne target" $ Perception Skill: the attacker scores higher MoS than the target, the target surprised. If the Stealth Skill Check fails, surprise is still achieved long as the target also failed his Perception Check; Since the target iS unaware of the attack his perception does not figure into the TN to hit him_ Use only the lower of the target" INT or RFL as the base TN to hit surprised target: Sixth Sense: Characters with the Sixth Sense Trait are dif- ficult to surprise Their TN for the Perception Test to detect sur- prise moditied by
ZERO GRAvITY Zero gravity (correctly termed microgravity) is disorienting to anyone not acquainted with its effects; Normal perceptions of up and down do not apply because there is no downward force to provide orientation and the ight of people floating at varied angles often disturbs the inexperienced_ The Zero-G Operations Skill reflects the experience and training gained the crew of   JumpShips and   DropShips. Characters with the skill may operate in zero-G conditions with little Or no penalty. Characters without the skill, however, must add Modifier to the target number of any Action Check they make zero gravity environments Characters without the Zero G Operations Skill must aiso roll 206 whenever they first enter zero gravity environment: If the result less than 6, tne character suffers space sick: ness and +1 penalty applies to all Skill Checks while in any zero-gravity environment from this point forward. Zero gravity conditions affect the movement of all charac ters. Those without the Zero G Operations Skill move at one- tenth their normal speed_ Characters with the Zero G Operations Skill move at half their normal rate Firing recoil-inducing weapons in zero gravity poses prob- lems as well. Consider laser; PPC gyrojet. needler and bow Kea pons as recoilless for this purpose, All other weapons are recoil inducing: ) Any character firing recoil-inducing weapon without bracing himself against bulkhead other solid Sur face (using Simple Action) must make a RFL Check: If the roll tails the recoil of the weapon propels the character backward The character strikes nearby bulkhead ad suffers 206 melee damage (AP 01.
SUBDUING ATTACKS Sometimes an attacker may not want to kill or even injure target; but rather cause nim pain attempt t0 force sur- render; or perhaps knock him unconscious_ subduing attack resolved like normai attack, but nas no cnance intlicting wounds (though it does still cause Fatigue). Instead successful hit will force the target to make an immediate Knockout Check, with all the usual modifiers plus additional modifier equal to the wound value of the type of wound the attack would have inflicted_ For example, if the attack would have inflicted Minor Wound  the target would take Fatigue and would make Knockout Check with a additional +2 Modifier Some weapons_ such as stunsticks always attack t0 sub- due_ This characteristic listed the Notes column on the Weapon Table (p. 153}.
